title: ranunculus pensylvanicus subsp. riparius
intro: Ranunculus pensylvanicus subsp. riparius, also known as Pennsylvania buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and anti-cancer properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus pensylvanicus subsp. riparius include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and potentially inhibit the growth of cancer cells. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
